chunk_summary: This section outlines a structured 24-hour recall method for assessing diet adequacy in children with chronic undernutrition, emphasizing energy, protein targets, and dietary diversity to guide interventions based on adequacy ratios.
learning_objectives: Understand the use of 24-hour recall for rapid diet adequacy assessment in chronic undernutrition.
Learn to calculate and interpret the diet adequacy ratio to guide nutritional interventions.
Identify strategies to improve dietary diversity and nutrient density in food-insecure settings.
Recognize the importance of addressing infection burden and feeding dynamics in cases of poor growth despite adequate intake.
key_facts: If the adequacy ratio is <75%, prioritize securing a minimum meal structure (3 meals + snacks) and adding a consistent protein source daily.
For ratios of 75–90%, focus on nutrient density by replacing sugary beverages and ultraprocessed snacks with protein-rich snacks and fruits/vegetables.
Ratios >90% with poor growth velocity require investigation of infection burden, enteric exposure, or chronic disease red flags.
Dietary diversity should be assessed using the ICMR-NIN food group framework, documenting missing groups for follow-up.
reference_quote: "Use a structured 24-hour recall, recorded meal-by-meal, then compare what the child actually consumed to the calculated energy and protein targets." (Dietary Guidelines for Indians 2024)
document_key_topics: Nutritional interventions based on adequacy ratios
